  3. Largest Cities In The US By Population
2025-04-01

Largest Cities In The US By Population

The United States has hundreds of cities spread across 50 states, ranging in population from a few thousand to over eight million. New York City is the largest with approximately 8.3 million residents across its five boroughs. Los Angeles follows with about 3.8 million, and Chicago is third with roughly 2.7 million. Houston and Phoenix round out the top five. Each of these major metropolitan areas has unique address patterns, ZIP code distributions, and area code assignments.

New York City alone has hundreds of ZIP codes, with Manhattan using codes starting with 100 and 101, Brooklyn using 112, Queens using 113 and 114, the Bronx using 104, and Staten Island using 103. The city uses area codes 212, 646, and 917 for Manhattan, and 718 and 347 for the outer boroughs. Los Angeles uses area codes 213 and 323 for the central city, with 310, 424, and 818 covering surrounding areas. Chicago uses 312 and 773 depending on location. Understanding this relationship between cities, area codes, and ZIP codes is important for generating realistic addresses that pass validation checks. Our city address generator maintains these relationships automatically.

Beyond the top five, other major cities include San Antonio, San Diego, Dallas, San Jose, Austin, Jacksonville, Fort Worth, Columbus, Charlotte, Indianapolis, Seattle, Denver, Washington DC, Nashville, and Portland. Each of these cities has its own ZIP code ranges and area codes. For example, Seattle uses area code 206, Denver uses 303 and 720, and Nashville uses 615. When testing address collection forms, it is valuable to generate addresses from multiple cities to ensure your system handles geographic diversity correctly.

Population size directly affects the number of ZIP codes a city has. Larger cities need more ZIP codes to serve their populations efficiently. New York City has hundreds, while a small town might have just one. Our random address generator draws from a broad database of cities and ZIP codes, producing addresses from both major metropolitan areas and smaller towns. If you need addresses from a specific state, our state-specific address pages let you focus on one state at a time, generating addresses that are consistent with that state's cities and ZIP code patterns.

For developers testing location-based features, our ZIP code directory provides a comprehensive reference for codes across all cities. Our phone number generator assigns area codes that match each city's geographic region, ensuring complete consistency across all address fields. If you need names and emails to accompany city-specific addresses, our random person generator creates complete profiles with consistent city and state assignments.

Understanding the relationship between city populations, ZIP code density, and area code distribution helps developers build more accurate and reliable applications. Our random address generator produces addresses that respect all of these geographic relationships. For developers working on location-based features, our tool collection provides everything from individual phone number generation to complete person profiles, all with consistent geographic data.

From the largest metros to small towns, understanding city populations and their geographic patterns helps developers build better applications. Our address generator produces realistic addresses from cities of all sizes, ensuring your test data reflects the diversity of real-world US addresses. For comprehensive testing, pair it with our test address generator.
